summary: Closed the cross-file call resolution gap identified in M10 for 8 languages (c, cpp, ruby, kotlin, swift, php, luau, ocaml). The canonical 2-file fixture has `main` calling both `helper` (intra-file) and `b_util` (cross-file). Before VAL-011, those 8 languages reported 1 edge instead of 2 — silently passing M10's permissive `total_edges >= 1` threshold. M11 tightens the matrix-test assertion to `total_edges >= 2`, then fixes each language so the assertion holds. After VAL-011, all 18 languages return >= 2 edges. 234 of 234 matrix tests pass; 4737 of 4737 core-lib tests pass; clippy clean.

matrix.calls_row_before: 10 OK + 8 WEAK (c, cpp, ruby, kotlin, swift, php, luau, ocaml — 1 edge instead of 2)

test_changes.rationale: Canonical fixture promises 2 edges (main→helper intra + main→b_util cross-file). Tightened assertion forces handlers to actually emit cross-file edges instead of silently dropping them.

c.root_cause: Direct call falls through resolve_call(CallType::Direct) — no import_map entry, no class match.
c.fix_location: crates/tldr-core/src/callgraph/resolution.rs:1497-1547 (new resolve_global_free_function helper) + Direct-call fallback hook at resolution.rs:781-789
c.approach: Global cross-file free-function fallback. Accepts unique cross-file match; deduplicates by (file_path, line) to handle multi-key indexing.

cpp.approach: Same global free-function fallback (gated on language list including 'cpp' and 'c++').

ruby.root_cause: Direct call; no class to resolve through.

kotlin.idiom: fun bUtil(): Int = 2 (in Util.kt); bUtil() (in Main.kt, same package)
kotlin.root_cause: No explicit `import` in source (same-package functions); Direct call falls through.

swift.idiom: func bUtil() -> Int (in Util.swift); bUtil() (in Main.swift, same module)
swift.root_cause: Same as Kotlin — no source-level import for same-module bareword.

php.root_cause: Same Direct-call fallthrough. Note: handler emits a quirky ImportDef alias='once' but this doesn't affect resolution.

luau.root_cause: Two issues: (1) Luau handler did not extract LHS variable name as ImportDef.alias, so module_imports keyed under './util' instead of 'util', causing receiver lookup to miss. (2) module_index lua/luau aliases didn't include './<module>' form (defense-in-depth).
luau.fix_location: crates/tldr-core/src/callgraph/languages/luau.rs:236-282 (new extract_aliased_require) + luau.rs:618-650 (parse_imports two-pass) + module_index.rs:496-518 (extended lua/luau aliases)
luau.approach: Two-pass parse_imports (modeled on lua handler): first walks variable_declarations to bind alias from `local X = require('Y')`, second walks function_call for standalone requires.

ocaml.idiom: Util.b_util () (in main.ml; sibling util.ml provides the module — no `open` needed)
ocaml.root_cause: module_imports empty (no parsed import); receiver 'Util' doesn't match func_index key 'util' (lowercase from path_to_module_ocaml). Standard receiver-lookup chain misses.
ocaml.approach: (1) Module index aliases `util.ml` under both 'util' and 'Util' so reverse_lookup is unambiguous. (2) resolve_call_with_receiver adds an OCaml-specific fallback that lowercases the receiver (matching the func_index Python-style key) before declining.

files_unchanged: crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/mod.rs (canonical fixtures unchanged — same 2-file/3-function/2-edge contract that M10 used; the test assertion changed, not the fixture)

verification.matrix_tests: cargo test -p tldr-cli --test language_command_matrix → 234 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored
verification.core_lib_tests: cargo test -p tldr-core --lib → 4737 passed, 0 failed, 356 ignored (pre-existing)

out_of_scope_deferred.ruby_bareword_calls: Tracked in M10 capability section. Not addressed here per VAL-011 contract — fixture continues to use `helper()` with parens as M10's workaround.
out_of_scope_deferred.php_require_once_parse_quirk: ImportDef emits alias='once' for `require_once`. Cosmetic in `tldr imports` output but doesn't affect calls resolution after VAL-011's bareword fallback. Defer to a future milestone.
